Call 2025 - Round 1
Mobility of young people (KA152-YOU)
Form ID: KA152-YOU-13D47DC1 Deadline (Brussels Time): 12 Feb 2025 12:00:00
Project Summary
Please provide short answers to the following questions to summarise the information you have provided in the rest of the
application form.
Please use full sentences and clear language. In case your project is accepted, the summary you provided will be made
public by the European Commission and the National Agencies.
What do you want to achieve by implementing the project? What are the objectives of your project? Please specify from the
perspective of youth work practice.
The primary objective of this project is to empower young people with practical tools and strategies to enhance their
emotional intelligence and foster inclusion. Emotional intelligence -including self-awareness, self-regulation, motivation,
empathy, and social skills- is a critical skill set for navigating diverse social environments and addressing interpersonal
challenges. By strengthening these abilities, participants will be better equipped to build inclusive communities, promote
mutual understanding, and effectively respond to the emotional and social complexities of modern life.
Through workshops and interactive activities, participants will practice applying emotional intelligence principles in realistic
scenarios. For instance, exercises like “SWOT analysis” will help them assess personal strengths and challenges, while
activities such as “Mindfulness meditation” and “Walk in My Shoes” will develop their ability to empathize with others and
regulate emotions. These methods ensure that participants leave with both theoretical understanding and practical
experience, directly applicable to their personal lives and community engagement efforts.
Simultaneously, the project will emphasize the development of essential soft skills, including teamwork and communication,
through interactive activities such as “Story Circle” and “Human Bingo.” These exercises will encourage participants to
articulate their thoughts and emotions clearly while collaborating effectively in diverse teams.
Beyond emotional intelligence, the project aligns with the broader objectives of youth work by promoting several key
competencies:
Cultural Awareness and Expression: Participants will engage in intercultural teamwork and international evenings, fostering
dialogue and mutual understanding between cultures.
Multilingualism: By communicating across linguistic boundaries, participants will enhance their ability to interact in both
native and foreign languages.
Personal, Social, and Learning Competence: Through non-formal educational methods, participants will face real-world
challenges, building resilience and adaptability.
Entrepreneurship: Activities aimed at designing and implementing inclusion-related projects will encourage initiative and
creativity.
Digital Skills: Participants will use technology to create content, collaborate online, and promote the project, strengthening
their digital literacy.
Citizenship Competence: By encouraging participants to take action in their communities, the project supports the
development of proactive, socially conscious individuals.
Ultimately, this project aims to inspire participants to become ambassadors of inclusion and emotional intelligence. It
provides them with the tools to thrive in diverse environments, act as change-makers in their communities, and contribute to
building a more inclusive society. Through this initiative, we address critical aspects of youth work, empowering young
people to lead with empathy and initiative in a rapidly changing world.
What activities do you plan to implement? What is the number and profile of the participants involved?
This initiative aims to bring together young individuals from diverse backgrounds to explore the role of emotional intelligence
in promoting inclusion and social harmony. The program is designed with a range of dynamic workshops, interactive
discussions, and collaborative exercises, while each day and session of the program follows a thematic structure.
The youth exchange will engage in total 32 young people aged 18 to 30, hailing from 4 different countries, with each group
consisting of 8 participants plus one group leader. Leaders, who must be at least 18 years old and have prior experience in
youth work or Erasmus+ projects, will guide and support their teams throughout the exchange. All participants are required
to have at least a B1 level of English proficiency and exhibit a strong interest in emotional intelligence and inclusion-focused
activities. Lastly, gender balance will be prioritized through the selection process, by encouraging equal participation of all
genders within each group.
Below are described the main activities planned:
Picture Language
Participants decide on 5 important topics to illustrate about themselves (favorite food, music) and draw them on A4 paper
without any words. They’re then split into small groups, where each person explains their drawings but in their mother
tongue! The others guess what each drawing represents, based on the visuals and tone of language alone, creating a fun
challenge and cultivating intercultural connections.
Expectation & Commitment Art Collage
Participants create in groups, a collective art collage representing their expectations and commitments. Using magazines &
newspapers, they collect images and words that resonate with their hopes for the group experience. After assembling their
collage on a large poster, the group discusses the significance of each element and how it reflects their shared vision.
Mental health professional visit

Call 2025 - Round 1
Mobility of young people (KA152-YOU)
Form ID: KA152-YOU-13D47DC1 Deadline (Brussels Time): 12 Feb 2025 12:00:00
A licensed mental health professional will be joining to lead a session focused on understanding and strengthening
emotional intelligence. Participants will explore the essential components of EQ -self-awareness, self-regulation, motivation,
empathy, and social skills- and learn practical strategies to develop these skills in their personal and professional lives.
Empathy Mapping
Participants get divided into groups and provide each with a real-life inclusion-related scenario. Each group uses an
empathy map to consider what this person might be thinking, feeling, saying, and doing in the scenario. Groups then share
their insights with everyone, highlighting how understanding different perspectives can lead to more inclusive actions.
What's your values?
Participants will identify their core values by ranking a list of potential values (e.g., honesty, family). They will reflect on how
these values influence their decisions and behaviors. After individual work, participants can discuss how awareness of their
values helps them make choices aligned with their true selves, for deeper self-understanding and intentional living.
Mindfulness Meditation
Participants will engage in a guided mindfulness meditation focused on breath awareness. This practice encourages them
to observe their thoughts and emotions without judgment, promoting present-moment awareness. Later, participants will
share their experiences and discuss how mindfulness can help manage stress and regulate emotions.
Goal ladder
Participants select a personal or professional goal and create a "goal ladder" by breaking it down into smaller, manageable
steps. They will list each step from the bottom (starting point) to the top (goal achieved) and add what motivates them at
each stage. This process helps participants visualize progress and stay motivated by focusing on small, achievable steps.
Walk in My Shoes
Each participant is assigned a unique character -young woman facing workplace discrimination/ teenager grappling with
anxiety. For one hour, they embody their character, exploring their thoughts and emotions while engaging in discussions and
activities. Then, they reflect on their experiences, gaining insights into the challenges of discrimination. Afterward, the team
gathers to share reflections, fostering empathy and understanding of diverse experiences.
Perspective Switch
In pairs, participants share a personal opinion on a neutral topic. Then, each partner must explain the other's perspective as
if it were their own. This helps develop active listening and understanding of differing viewpoints.
DIY Workshop Creation & Presentation
Building on what they've learned throughout the week, participants will work in small groups to design their own workshops.
Each group will select one key element of EQ and create a brief, interactive session aimed at teaching or practicing this skill,
with guidance from the facilitators. After preparing, teams will present their workshops to the larger group and receive
constructive feedback on their outcome.
What results and impact do you expect your project to have?
“EQ for Inclusion” project is expected to generate a profound and lasting impact on multiple levels: on the participants, local
communities, partner organizations, and society at large. The outcomes will extend beyond tangible materials, deeply
influencing skill development, mindset shifts, and long-term engagement in inclusion-driven initiatives.
In the short term, we expect participants to experience significant personal growth as they develop key emotional
intelligence skills that will enable them to navigate social interactions with greater empathy, self-awareness, and emotional
resilience. Through experiential learning, group discussions, and intercultural activities, they will strengthen their ability to
recognize and regulate their emotions, actively listen to others, and respond constructively in diverse social and cultural
settings. These skills will allow participants to create more inclusive environments where individuals from different
backgrounds feel heard, respected, and valued. Moreover, by working in mixed teams on creative and reflective tasks,
participants will develop stronger collaboration, communication, and leadership abilities—essential for their future personal
and professional lives.
In the long term, we foresee the project having a lasting impact on participants and their wider communities. As they apply
emotional intelligence in their daily lives, they will be better equipped to navigate challenges, build stronger relationships,
and contribute to more inclusive and empathetic communities. The self-awareness and social awareness they gain will
empower them to become proactive agents of change, fostering understanding and respect in their interactions with others.
By spreading the principles of EQ in their personal, academic, and professional environments, we anticipate a ripple effect,
where the positive impact of the project extends beyond the immediate participants to their peers, families, and workplaces.
Beyond individual growth, this project is designed to have a broader societal impact by promoting emotional intelligence as
a foundation for mutual understanding and social cohesion. Through creative outputs such as presentations, group projects,
and artistic activities, participants will actively engage in sharing their learning with others. The dissemination of project
outcomes via social media, partner networks, and local communities will further amplify the message, ensuring that the
importance of emotional intelligence reaches a wider audience.
At an organizational level, the project will strengthen collaboration between the participating organizations, promoting long-
term partnerships focused on emotional intelligence, youth empowerment, and intercultural dialogue. The methods and best
practices developed will enhance the capacity of each partner organization to integrate EQ-focused approaches into their
youth work, ensuring a sustainable impact that continues beyond the project’s duration.
Aligned with the core values of Erasmus+, the project will also contribute to greater European cooperation by encouraging
intercultural learning, active citizenship, and the development of key competencies. Participants will leave with not only
enhanced emotional intelligence but also a greater appreciation for diversity and a commitment to creating inclusive spaces

Please briefly present your organisation/the group.
Square of Youth Association (SOYA) is a diverse group of young people brought together by Erasmus+ projects. Many of us
have transformed our lives through these projects, developing key competencies, soft skills, and forming connections with

friends across Europe, among many other benefits. We are dedicated to delivering youth work at a quality we would expect
from ourselves, believing that the best way to engage in youth work is through young people working with young people. We
are truly an Erasmus+ generation at Square of Youth Association.
Our mission is to connect Hungarian youth with Europe. We aim to provide opportunities for them to explore Europe,
connect with their European peers, and empower them to become active citizens. We encourage them to take initiatives and
contribute to their communities.
We also aim and working to:
● Help young people learn through non-formal learning activities and enhance their soft skills. Increasing youth participation
through our projects and providing international opportunities for young people.
● Connect youth with stakeholders and decision-makers, facilitating the translation of youth voices to them and increasing
youth involvement.
● Promote dialogue among young people from EU member states and candidate countries, which includes collaborating
with partners from outside the EU as well.
Internationality is a key component of our activities because we believe that international opportunities and mobilities have
the potential to improve young people's learning and growth on a high level.
We primarily focus on three key topics, but due to our diversity and ambition, we are open to many other areas as well. The
three key topics are:
● Entrepreneurship and empowering young people to become entrepreneurs. Several of our members became
entrepreneurs at a young age. We believe entrepreneurship not only as a business endeavor but also for its entrepreneurial
skills, which are great soft skills in many aspects of one’s life.
● Active citizenship and youth empowerment. Aiming for our projects and activities can have a multiplier effect and create
impact on a broader scale.
● Topics closely connected to the European Union and being a European citizen.
SOYA is also a member of the Danube Youth Organization Network and is committed to fostering cooperation among
youth-focused organizations in the Danube Region. We find this particularly important in facilitating meetings with decision-
makers and establishing cross-border collaborations.
What are the activities and experience of the organisation in youth work? Please provide information on your organisation’s
/ group’s regular youth work activities.
All the activities we implement are thought, planned and realized by young people.
Regarding the topic of entrepreneurship, we have won the "Entrepreneur in a Week" Erasmus+ youth exchange project,
which will be implemented in spring 2025. Participants will receive all the necessary skills and knowledge to become
entrepreneurs in an intensive week, such as business plan writing, pitching, understanding how to get funding for their
ideas, and more. The project also focuses on developing entrepreneurial skills.
We are currently developing a Visegrad Minigen project: a policy hackathon aimed at young people to explore pathways to
entrepreneurship and identify necessary supports. The project will culminate in a policy document to be presented to
decision-makers and stakeholders.
Integration is also an important topic for us. Currently, we are implementing a Solidarity project in Budapest where we bring
Hungarian and international university students closer together through board games and regular local events.
We organize Erasmus+ information sessions with the goal of democratizing access to Erasmus+. Youth exchanges serve as
a valuable introduction for many young people, yet there are numerous other project types within Erasmus+ – such as
Erasmus for Young Entrepreneurs or European Solidarity Corps (ESC) – that they may not be aware of.
In connection with this initiative, we were exhibiting at the Sziget Festival 2024 to engage naturally with young people,
speaking their language and promoting Erasmus+ and SOYA. Our goal is to attend more festivals to promote Erasmus+,
and non-formal learning, and to inform them about their opportunities.
In addition to our current projects, all of our members have previously participated in numerous Erasmus+ initiatives,
including youth exchanges, training courses, and Erasmus+ semesters at their university. Some of us have also taken part
in European Solidarity Corps volunteer programs, while others have engaged in Erasmus for Young Entrepreneurs. Before
choosing to become actively involved in the program, we were all well-integrated into the Erasmus community and deeply
passionate about its positive impact on our lives.

Background and experience
Please briefly present the organisation / the group
Hellenic Youth Participation (based in Athens, Greece) stands for intercultural dialogue, non-formal education, mutual
understanding and solidarity.
The main aim of the group is to promote human rights through non-formal education and experiential learning. We train
different age groups (teenagers, young adults, parents, youthworkers, facilitators, teachers) in order to inspire and stimulate
European citizens to become active parts of their local and European community. We wish to motivate them to become
involved and to raise awareness about current values like equal opportunities, social inclusion and cultural diversity.
Through our projects we aim at the promotion of non-formal differentiated learning and the importance of personal
development. HYP is active on a local, national and European level.
In 2020, our youth exchange “Youth Votes for Europe” and our EVS “Go Out, Bring In”, as well as in 2021, our ESC project
“Volunteers for Environment” were recognized as good practices by the Greek National Agency. In 2021, our youth initiative
“Solidarity for Fashion” was presented in the “EU Regions Week” as a good practice and in 2022, we were featured in
Eurodesk's and Eryica's guide on Youth Participation. We were also awarded the silver prize in the volunteer competition for
Greece for our contribution in education in 2016, 2017, 2018,2022.
What are the activities and experience of the partner organisation in youth work? Please provide information on your
organisation’s / group’s regular youth work activities at local level.
HYP stands for the values of human rights, democracy and active participation as they are promoted in the European Union,
the European Parliament and the Council of Europe.
PARTICIPATION IN EUROPEAN&GLOBAL YOUTH EVENTS
*HYP is the first Greek organization to facilitate workshops and send teams of young people to international conferences
such as the European Youth Event (2014-18) and the World Forum of Democracy (2014-17) in order to promote active
participation and give the opportunity to young people to familiarize themselves with the EU institutions. In the WFD of 2014,
HYP presented the workshop “Is possible non-formal education to empower democracy?” in Strasbourg.
MULTIPLYING THE KNOWLEDGE IN LOCAL LEVEL:
One of the core principles of HYP is to organize trainings and sessions with young people through non-formal education
methodology. In the field of democracy and human rights, we organize seminars and workshops to address the constant
raise of racism and stereotyping, the importance of active local participation and social inclusion in Athens.

Recent Local Events:
2021
“Say No to Domestic Violence”: campaign to raise awareness in Greece
“Dive In”: Creation of innovative LARP scenarios for youth in the danger of radicalization
“Democratize”: a KA3 project in collaboration with Greek NGOs about the participation of young people to democratic life
2020
“Supporting the young people during the COVID19 pandemic”: the organization has provided a wide range of workshops
about the idea of Europe, democracy and human rights to more than 250 young people in Greece. By creating these small
groups, we are supporting young people to cope with the stress and isolation through as well as gain new skills.
“Power to Change”: local events against hate speech where young people created their own campaigns through creativity,
photography, music and digital skills
“Black Lives Matter”: our young people created a campaign bringing more awareness on systemic racism
“It’s All about Solidarity”: a ΚΑ2 project which promoted success stories of refugees and immigrants and their good
practices.
2019
“Escape Room of Human Rights”: our youth team organized 2 escape rooms of human rights in order to raise awareness on
the history of human rights and the refugee crisis. More than 150 young people participated in the event and spread the
importance of human rights today.
“LARP for a European Community”: our youthworkers created a new universe where superheroes were going to vote for
their representation in their elections. The young people got super powers and started acting like active citizens, they voted
for their party and experienced the consequences to their lives.
2018
“Hate Fighters in Action”: a long term project about fighting discrimination and hate speech through art (graphics, video,
music, photography), interactive tools and debate.
“People, Places, Partnerships”: in order to support active participation of young people we have created a team (18-30 yo)
which attempts to support social change based on the community needs of Athens.
“HYP International Relations”: is a team of young people that attempts to interpret the recent political events and research
on the way they affect our community.
PARTNERSHIPS WITH LOCAL STAKEHOLDERS
Our network of collaborations includes the Municipality of Athens, local schools, student youth organizations (ESN, Aegee,
Aiesec), organisations that support social inclusion of refugees, migrants, roma communities and global NGOs (Amnesty
International). We have frequent meetings to support their needs and create new local initiatives.
Please give information on the key staff/persons involved in this application and on the competences and previous
experience that they will bring to the project.
Eleni Kardamitsi is an experienced trainer, founder and head of the department of youth and non-formal education initiatives
at the Hellenic Youth Participation with 20 years of experience in the field of formal and non-formal education. She is a
member of the ToT (Training of Trainers), Salto network of trainers and the British Council. She has a multi-level
educational background, with a Bachelor of Arts in English Literature and Culture, an MA in Critical Cultural Theory and an
MBA - which focused on motivating volunteers in Generation Y and Z. She has experience in guiding and coordinating teen
groups, young adult groups, young professional groups, groups of trainers, educators and youth experts. Her specialization
is creativity, ecological consciousness, ecological art, botanical art and nature-based methodology in urban and rural areas.
Pablo Pandolfi is a physical education instructor with experience in multinational environment in Montevideo, Madrid,
Barcelona and Athens through work and volunteering. He was a volunteer in Special Olympics in 2011 and will be a Head of

Project Rationale
In this section, you are asked to explain the aims of your project, what you want to achieve and what are its expected
benefits on participants and participating organisations. Make sure to read the questions carefully and address all sub-
questions.
Needs and Objectives
Why do you want to carry out this project? Please describe the issues and needs you want to address and your project’s
objectives.
The Feel to Connect: EQ for Inclusion project recognizes emotional intelligence (EQ) as a transformative tool in fostering
inclusive environments where young people from diverse backgrounds can thrive and connect meaningfully. Within today’s
European communities, youth often face challenges of exclusion, misunderstanding, and conflict rooted in cultural
differences and limited interpersonal skills. These difficulties frequently stem from a lack of emotional awareness and an
inability to navigate complex social dynamics. Consequently, young people may struggle to form meaningful relationships,
leading to isolation. This project directly addresses these issues by equipping participants with essential competencies to
understand and manage their emotions while building strong connections with others.
At its core, the initiative is built on the belief that EQ is fundamental for promoting mutual respect, understanding, and
inclusion. Through a series of non-formal education activities, the program fosters the five key components of EQ: self-
awareness, self-regulation, motivation, empathy, and social skills.
The program begins with practical workshops and interactive sessions that guide participants in exploring their emotional
responses to different situations, developing Self-Awareness. By understanding how emotions shape interactions,
participants develop deeper self-reflection. This heightened awareness lays the foundation for all other EQ skills, enabling
participants to communicate more thoughtfully and intentionally.
In addition, through tailored exercises and group activities, participants practice emotional control, active listening, and
stress management. These self-regulation skills are vital for resolving conflicts, managing disappointments, and maintaining
emotional balance in personal and professional contexts.
To inspire personal growth, the program emphasizes goal-setting exercises and feedback sessions. Participants set
realistic, measurable goals for emotional development, gaining a sense of purpose and direction. Achieving these goals
enhances their self-confidence, and sense of motive.
Empathy is a central aspect to the project, and is going to be achieved through activities like culture nights, where
participants share their traditions, customs, and personal stories. These experiences deepen understanding of diverse
cultural perspectives, nurturing mutual respect.
Last but not least, the program includes structured reflection sessions where participants track their progress using tools like
Youthpass. These sessions encourage participants to document insights, identify areas for growth, and celebrate
achievements, reinforcing ownership of their personal development.
The Feel to Connect initiative has four primary objectives:
To develop participants' emotional intelligence as a foundation for creating inclusive spaces where individuals from diverse
backgrounds collaborate and connect respectfully.
To empower participants as changemakers by equipping them with EQ tools to bridge social and cultural divides. This will
enable them to become proactive agents of positive change in their communities.
To encourage mutual understanding and respect by providing shared experiences and intercultural learning opportunities.
To promote experiential outputs, such as group projects, presentations, and artistic activities, showcasing participants’ skills
and knowledge in creative ways.
By addressing these objectives, the project supports young people’s personal development and contributes to building
inclusive, resilient communities across Europe. Participants not only learn to manage their own emotions but also foster
empathy and collaboration, enabling them to challenge stereotypes, embrace diversity, and strengthen social bonds.
How does your project link to the objectives of the Erasmus programme and those of Youth Exchanges?
The project aligns both with the Erasmus+ and Youth Exchange’s objectives in various ways.
Initially, it promotes and raises awareness of inclusion and diversity, through a series of emotional intelligence workshops
designed to help participants identify, challenge, and overcome deep-rooted stereotypes and biases. These workshops will
focus on developing critical emotional and social skills that allow participants to create an environment of acceptance,
respect, and understanding for all individuals, regardless of their cultural or social background. In doing so, the project
seeks to create a culture where diversity is not just tolerated, but celebrated.
Furthermore, non-formal education methods are being utilized, with the project focusing on the participants’ practical skills,
knowledge, and values applicable to real-life situations. More specifically, activities such as “Perspective switch” and
“Blindfolded” will cultivate participants’ communication skills, essential for the every-day life, while activities such as
“Mindfulness Meditation” and “Windows & Mirrors” combined with the mental-health professional visit, will elevate the
participants’ personal growth and development. Finally, role-playing activities such as “Walk in my shoes” and “Scenario
role-playing” in addition to the “DIY workshop creation and presentation” will offer the participants the ideal opportunity to
get deeply engaged in interactive and knowledge-building activities and learning experiences. All these methods will not only
encourage collaboration, critical thinking, and problem-solving, but they will also deepen their understanding of diverse

Another alignment between this project and the Erasmus objectives is the promotion of intercultural dialogue and the sense
of European identity. “EQ for Inclusion” project brings together young people from a variety of European countries to engage
in collaborative projects with a European focus, and reflective discussions that encourage open exchange of ideas and
experiences. This process will help participants connect more deeply with both their own cultural identities and those of
others, while also encouraging a collective sense of European belonging, reinforcing the idea that Europe is not just a
political or geographical entity, but a community of diverse cultures, ideas, and histories.
Additionally, this project encourages active engagement in society and promotes participation in democratic life, by fostering
a strong sense of shared responsibility among participants and cultivating an awareness of their roles as active citizens
within the European community. The project will empower participants to take on leadership roles in decision-making
processes, both within their communities and in broader democratic structures. By providing them tools to understand and
navigate democratic processes, the project aims to inspire young people to become proactive change-makers, contributing
to the development of more inclusive, and democratic societies.
Last but not least, the link between this project and the Erasmus - Youth Exchanges objectives, can be found in the
advancement of core European values, such as equality, social cohesion, and cultural exchange. “EQ for Inclusion” will
create intentional opportunities for participants to engage with and reflect on these values in the context of their everyday
lives, encouraging them to recognize and address social inequalities, promote solidarity across national and cultural divides,
and understand the vital importance of mutual respect and understanding in building a cohesive European society.
Impact
How will your project benefit the young participants involved in the project, during and after the project lifetime?
This youth exchange project is designed to provide young participants with the tools, competences and knowledge
necessary to foster a more inclusive and empathetic society. Participants will be offered the opportunity to engage in a
transformative learning experience that will benefit them both during and long after the project concludes.
During the project:
Through the project, participants will develop a range of essential skills in emotional intelligence, which will enable them to
better navigate and contribute to diverse social environments. These skills include self-awareness and self-regulation,
motivation, empathy and social skills. By learning to recognize and understand their own emotions, as well as the emotions
of others, participants will become more effective in building relationships and fostering inclusive spaces. The ability for
cultural awareness will flourish as participants will explore diverse cultural perspectives, while literacy competence and
multilingualism will be developed as they will express ideas and communicate across languages. By addressing issues of
equity and diversity, citizenship competence will be nurtured, empowering youth to actively contribute to inclusive
communities, and through decision-making activities and structured, logical thinking, participants’ skills for entrepreneurship
and mathematics/STEM will be embraced.
At the same time, participants will engage in non-formal education methods, which will emphasize active, participatory
learning. Through interactive activities like group discussions and role-playing, personal, social, and learning to learn
competences will be strengthened, while through the use of digital tools for storytelling and virtual collaboration activities,
participants will improve their digital skills. Throughout the project, participants will engage with the Youthpass framework,
which will serve as a self-evaluation tool, helping participants track their personal development and achievements
throughout the project.
After the project:
With the end of the project, participants will have the opportunity to further embrace the skills learned in the project,
(empathy, self-awareness, social skills, etc) as this exchange will mark the starting point of a journey for personal growth.
The competences and knowledge gained will allow participants to consider and implement positive changes in their daily
lives. Whether it’s resolving conflicts, promoting inclusivity, or fostering empathy within their local communities, participants
will be able to apply their emotional intelligence in a variety of contexts. They will be more aware and effective in their
personal relationships and become advocates for inclusion, social justice, and equality. This experience will contribute not
only to the personal growth of participants but also their professional development and employability.
Another key benefit of this project is the opportunity for participants to establish international connections. Through
collaboration with peers from different countries, participants will expand their networks and form lasting friendships. These
connections will extend beyond the project itself, as participants will be encouraged to stay in touch through social media,
follow-up exchanges, and potential future projects.
In conclusion, this project offers participants a unique opportunity to develop critical skills and competence while fostering
intercultural understanding and social inclusion. The experiences, knowledge, and relationships gained during the project
will not only benefit participants during the event but will also continue to positively impact their lives long after it ends.
How will your project benefit the organisations or the groups of young people implementing the project, during and after the
project lifetime?
This project will bring significant benefits to the organizations involved in implementing it, both during its active phase and
beyond its lifetime. The organizations will not only expand their impact on a local level, but they will also get enriched by
implementing and promoting a cultural exchange, enhancing skills, expanding networks, and increasing access to resources

for inclusion, and contribute to the broader Erasmus+ mission of fostering solidarity, respect, and understanding across
borders.
What would be the impact of your project beyond the participants and participating organisations, at local, regional,
national, if any European level ?
The “EQ for inclusion” project, aims to create a ripple effect that extends beyond the immediate participants and partner
organizations, and promotes a positive change at local, regional, national, and European levels.
At the Local Level: Participants will communicate about and spread the Erasmus+ action, becoming ambassadors for
Erasmus+ and advocate for the importance of emotional intelligence in creating inclusive communities. They will share their
experiences and learning outcomes with friends, family, and via social media platforms such as Instagram, Facebook and
LinkedIn, effectively disseminating the project’s core messages. Through mandatory dissemination activities, we will
encourage participants to highlight the benefits of EQ and inclusion, reaching other young people who were not directly
involved in the project. By applying their newly developed EQ skills in their communities, participants will foster greater
understanding, empathy, and collaboration, inspiring others and creating a multiplier effect that could lead to long-term
cultural shifts in their local environments.
At the Regional Level: As part of the project implementation, participants will engage in various ways with the local
community. As an outcome, the region will be benefited, firstly because the Erasmus+ action along with the values of
inclusion will be promoted by the participants-locals interaction, and secondly because locals will get exposed to different
cultural perspectives, which could broaden their horizons. The project will further support the regional economy by sourcing
materials and services from small local businesses, contributing to economic and social well-being in the host area.
At the European Level: Project outputs such as training materials, guides, and multimedia resources will be made widely
accessible on platforms like SALTO and our website. These resources will serve as valuable tools for European youth
workers and organizations, inspiring similar initiatives across the continent.
The intercultural nature of the project will shape participants’ perspectives, reinforcing the importance of diversity and
inclusion while strengthening their sense of belonging to a shared European identity. At the same time, the partnerships
formed will pave the way for a long-term European impact, as they are intended to last long after the duration of the project,
to collectively achieve the goals of Erasmus+.

Selection Process: Participants will be chosen through open calls managed by partner organizations in each country. The
selection process will emphasize on motivation, interest in the project’s themes (Emotional intelligence and Inclusion), and
the willingness to actively engage. Each group will also include at least two participant with fewer opportunities, especially
individuals facing socio-economic or social -linked to discrimination- challenges. Special efforts will be made to
accommodate their needs, including assistance with travel arrangements, and other requirements. More specifically, upon
request, travel tickets and any special needs, will be addressed by our organization to facilitate their participation, while
participants will be asked in advance about their needs, so that they can be provided accordingly.
Each team will be led by a group leader, selected based on their experience in youth work or familiarity with Erasmus+
programs. Group leaders will play a pivotal role in supporting their teams, facilitating reflection sessions, ensuring effective
communication, and acting as liaisons between participants and organizers.
Το promote gender equality, we will maintain a balanced number of male and female participants in each national group, a
guideline clearly communicated to all partner organizations. Furthermore, groups will include participants of varying
experience levels and ages (18–30 for participants and 18+ for group leaders), facilitating peer learning and mentorship
within teams.
Please describe the role and involvement of the participants from each participating group in all phases (planning before
submitting application, preparation, implementation of activities and follow-up).
Planning Phase: Participants have been actively involved from the earliest stages of the project. To identify the focus of the
exchange, a needs assessment was conducted among our organization’s members and volunteers and through surveys,
open discussions and brainstorming, young people expressed their desire to explore the thematic of emotional intelligence
and how it can be valuable to promote inclusion. We got a total of 25 answers for this assessment and helped us to shape
the project’s timetable and content. Furthermore, this project is a true reflection of youth-driven innovation, as it has been
entirely developed by the young members of our organization.
Preparation Phase: During preparations, participants will take on both practical and collaborative roles. They will manage
travel arrangements, such as researching and booking their tickets. Partner organizations will organize online preparatory
meetings with their national groups to familiarize participants with the program’s objectives and logistics, clarify the
participants’ & team leaders’ responsibilities and discuss other important details, such as what is needed from participants
to bring with them and how they can be better prepared for the project. Additionally, participants in their national teams will
prepare for their intercultural evening, showcasing their countries through presentations, cultural artifacts, and food.
Implementation Phase: The implementation phase focuses on supporting active engagement and meaningful contributions
from participants. Each activity is designed to encourage interaction, collaboration, and feedback. Participants will
contribute by leading energizers and icebreakers, ensuring the group remains energized and cohesive. They will also play
an active role in group discussions, reflection sessions, and activity evaluations. Reflection sessions, both group-based and
individual, allow participants to share their experiences and insights, helping shape the ongoing program. Additionally,
participants will be encouraged to take leadership roles in certain activities, enhancing their confidence and organizational
skills. Most importantly, a whole day will be devoted to workshops created by the participants themselves, where they will
design and implement activities based on their own experiences, knowledge acquired and creative ideas. This approach will
not only strengthen their sense of ownership but will also provide an opportunity to put into practice the skills they acquired
during the exchange. Facilitators will offer guidance throughout to ensure the workshops are impactful and inclusive for all.
Follow-Up Phase: After the exchange, participants will play a crucial role in disseminating the project outcomes. They will
share their experiences through testimonials, photos, and videos on social media for their local organization. Participants will
also complete evaluation forms and their own YouthPass certificates, reflecting on their personal growth and learning
achievements. To maintain the connections built during the exchange, WhatsApp and Facebook groups will be transitioned
into alumni networks, laying the foundation for a collaboration and communication among the participants that will continue
long after the end of the project. These platforms will also serve as a space for participants to exchange ideas and explore
future projects together.
What will the participants learn about the chosen topic of the activity? Which learning outcomes or competences (i.e.
knowledge, skills and attitudes/behaviours) are to be acquired/improved by participants in the activity?
This project offers a transformative experience for participants, enabling them to develop essential skills, knowledge, and
attitudes that promote emotional intelligence and inclusion. At the same time they will be cultivating a great variety of soft
skills such as teamwork, and communication but also competencies such as cultural awareness and multilingualism.
First of all, participants will be exploring the core components of emotional intelligence - self-awareness, self-regulation,
motivation, empathy, and social skills- through five separate sessions, each one specifically designed and dedicated to each
component. Therefore, in the first session, Self-awareness will be analyzed and practiced, through activities such as
“SWOT analysis”, “What’s your values” and “Feelings check in”. In the next session, “Mindfulness meditation” and
“Scenario role playing” will set the ideal conditions for the skill of Self-regulation to be developed. Later, the participants’
Motivation will be tested with activities such as “Goal ladder”, “Fuel from the past” and “Words to live by”, while in the next
session, the cultivation of Empathy will be the main goal to be achieved, through the “Walk in my shoes” role play. Lastly,
Social skills of participants will be developed through the activities “Perspective switch” and “Blindfolded”.
At the same time, team building activities and ice-brakers such as “say the name”, “story circle”, “2 truths, 1 lie”, “human
bingo” and many others, will play an important role in the development of teamwork and communication skills, with

As for key competencies, the project promotes the following:
Cultural Awareness and Expression: The project will offer numerous opportunities for participants to learn about and
understand other cultures, as well as foster dialogue between them. This includes extensive international teamwork and
intercultural evenings.
Multilingualism: Participants will develop their communication skills both in their native languages and in foreign languages.
This way, participants will be enabled to interact effectively across linguistic and cultural boundaries.
Personal, Social, and Learning Competence: This project follows the non-formal educational method, and therefore
participants will practice on facing actively real time conditions and challenges, which will allow them to develop new skills.
Entrepreneurship: Participants will enhance their sense of initiative and entrepreneurship by designing and implementing
projects aimed at tackling inclusion-related challenges.
Digital skills: Technology plays a key role in this project, from online collaboration tools to social media platforms.
Participants will learn to use digital tools for project promotion, such as creating content to share their experiences and raise
awareness about inclusion.
Citizenship Competence: Participants will be encouraged to seize opportunities beyond the project and take initiatives within
their communities.
Overall, the project provides a holistic developmental experience, with participants acquiring specific skills and knowledge
but also developing the attitudes and behaviors necessary to thrive in diverse, inclusive environments.
What are the basic elements of the activity? Please describe at the very least the venue(s), non formal learning methods
used, aims of the session etc.
The venue where the project will be implemented is the Szilvásvárad Youth Hostel. This location offers an ideal combination
of natural beauty and practicality, making it perfectly suited for our program.
The youth hostel is located in Szilvásvárad, a charming village known for its picturesque surroundings and proximity to the
Szalajka Valley. Participants will have the opportunity to explore the area, enjoy outdoor activities, and connect with nature.
The hostel provides comfortable rooms with multiple beds, allowing participants to share accommodations sorted by
gender. Its facilities include outdoor spaces for activities, ensuring a dynamic and engaging program.
The accommodation can host up to 50 people, providing enough capacity for our group while ensuring privacy, as this setup
guarantees a quiet environment where participants can focus and interact without external disturbances.
The accommodation has its own restaurant, so meals will be provided on site, while the kitchen can accommodate various
dietary requirements, including gluten-free, lactose-free, and vegan options, ensuring all participants are catered to.
The venue is easily accessible, with Szilvásvárad being well-connected by public transport. Participants are encouraged to
use green transportation options such as trains or buses, which align with the sustainable values of our program. This
ensures convenient and environmentally friendly travel for all attendees.
All the sessions are based on non-formal education and are connected to Emotional Intelligence and Inclusion. The following
is a brief list containing the most important activities of the youth exchange.
Picture Language: Participants decide on 5 important topics to illustrate about themselves (favorite food, music) and draw
them on A4 paper without any words. They’re then split into small groups, where each person explains their drawings but in
their mother tongue! The others guess what each drawing represents, based on the visuals and tone of language alone.
Expecτation & Commitment Art Collage: Participants create in groups, a collective art collage representing their
expectations and commitments. After assembling their collage on a large poster, the group discusses the significance of
each element and how it reflects their shared vision.
Mental health professional visit: A licensed mental health professional will be joining to lead a session focused on
understanding and strengthening emotional intelligence. Participants will explore the essential components of EQ and learn
practical strategies to develop these skills in their lives.
Empathy Mapping: Participants get divided into groups and provide each with a real-life inclusion-related scenario. Each
group uses an empathy map to consider what this person might be thinking, feeling, saying, and doing in the scenario.
Groups then share their insights with everyone.
What's your values?: Participants will identify their core values by ranking a list of potential values. They will reflect on how
these values influence their decisions and behaviors. After individual work, participants can discuss how awareness of their
values helps them make choices aligned with their true selves, for deeper self-understanding and intentional living.
Mindfulness Meditation: Participants will engage in a guided mindfulness meditation focused on breath awareness. Later,
participants will share their experiences and discuss how mindfulness can help manage stress and regulate emotions.
Goal ladder: Participants select a personal or professional goal and create a "goal ladder" by breaking it down into smaller,
manageable steps. They will list each step from the bottom to the top and add what motivates them at each stage. This
process helps participants visualize progress and stay motivated by focusing on small, achievable steps.
Walk in My Shoes: Each participant is assigned a unique character -young woman facing workplace discrimination/
teenager grappling with anxiety. For one hour, they embody their character, exploring their thoughts and emotions while
engaging in discussions and activities. Then, they reflect on their experiences, gaining insights into the challenges of
discrimination. Afterward, the team gathers to share reflections, cultivating empathy and understanding of diverse
experiences.
Perspective Switch: In pairs, participants share a personal opinion on a neutral topic. Then, each partner must explain the
other's perspective as if it were their own. This helps develop active listening and understanding of differing viewpoints.

How will the groups of participants cooperate and communicate between them to prepare and follow-up on the Youth
Exchange?
From the earliest stages of preparation, the project emphasizes effective communication and collaboration among
participants to ensure a cohesive and impactful experience. Communication will unfold across several phases, combining
digital tools and face-to-face interactions.
In the preparation phase, WhatsApp will be a central communication tool. Two dedicated groups will be created: one for the
organizers to streamline coordination and another for participants. Through this platform, participants will introduce
themselves, share ideas, and engage in initial conversations around the project’s theme. In addition, WhatsApp will be an
important communication tool during the project as well, as it will be used to ensure the swift dissemination of information,
and facilitate the project implementation.
Online meetings will complement these interactions, providing a space for deeper engagement and better organization.
Organizers will first meet with partner organizations to coordinate responsibilities and logistics. Following this, participants
will join an online preparatory session designed to facilitate team introductions, plan cultural activities, and inform on other
important details.
As the exchange begins, in-person communication will take center stage. Participants will immerse themselves in interactive
workshops with team-building exercises and dynamic group discussions. Daily reflection sessions will encourage
participants to share their experiences, thoughts, and emotions, while special activities like national evenings will further
enrich the experience. Most importantly, participants will have the opportunity to deepen their communication by designing
and presenting their own unique workshops, drawing on the knowledge they’ve gained. Following the presentations, each
team will receive constructive feedback to further enhance their learning and growth.
Beyond the exchange itself, communication will remain a priority. Through Email, participants will have access to critical
information, from logistical details to reminders about post-project deliverables. A Facebook group will also serve as a lively
space for sharing updates, photos, and project-related resources. Here, participants can celebrate their achievements,
exchange insights, and continue the conversations sparked during the exchange.
Finally, Google Drive will act as a central repository for the project’s outputs, from photos and videos created during the
exchange to reflective exercises and project-related documents. This shared space will enable participants to easily access
and share their work while ensuring that valuable resources remain available for future use.

Project Design
Preparation, support and follow-up
How will you prepare the participants before the start of the activity (e.g. intercultural, linguistic, risk-prevention etc.) and
how will you support them during and after the activity?
Before the Start of the Project: To ensure the project runs smoothly, there will be an initial meeting organized by us, Square
of Youth Association, with all partner organizations. This meeting will cover issues such as: methodologies, the participant
selection process, potential risks, logistical details, responsibilities, and a review of the timetable. By aligning on these
elements, we aim to avoid confusion and ensure all partners are equipped to effectively respond to participant inquiries.ú
The participant selection process will be transparent, inclusive, and structured in three phases: a) recruitment: A call for
participants will be shared via social media, partner websites, and other platforms, b) evaluation and selection: Candidates
will be assessed based on criteria such as background diversity (academic, social, and geographical), motivation, language
skills, and an interest in intercultural and media-related learning. Special emphasis will be placed on ensuring a balanced
mix of ages, genders, and experiences, and c) communication: once selected, participants will receive detailed information
via an infopack, including essential project details, travel arrangements, and expectations.
Engaging Participants During Preparation: Selected participants will play an active role in shaping the project. Two weeks
before the preparatory online meeting (scheduled one month before the exchange), participants will fill out a form capturing
their initial reflections, interests and knowledge of the project themes. This data will help tailor workshop content to their
needs.
During the preparatory meeting, the partner organizations and team leaders will be introduced, helping participants connect
and understand the project’s European dimension. Then, important details about the project will be provided, such as
logistical arrangements, while practical guidance will be offered, in fields like the preparation of cultural presentations, and
the enhancement of the English vocabulary. Last but not least, the project’s infopack will be distributed and discussed,
containing detailed logistical and behavioral guidelines, travel instructions, and emergency contacts.
During the Activities: This project, and therefore all its sessions, is designed to accommodate participants with varying
levels of prior knowledge, experience and skills on emotional intelligence, offering both theoretical foundations and practical
applications. This approach ensures everyone can participate confidently. At the same time, plenty of ice-breaker activities
will be utilized so that communication, teamwork, and a sense of community can be promoted. Finally, daily reflection
sessions will provide participants a chance to share their thoughts, evaluate their experiences, and -very importantly-
provide feedback, which facilitators will use to refine upcoming activities.
After the Activities: Post-project support will focus on maintaining connections and ensuring long-term impact. Participants
will fill out evaluation forms and complete their YouthPass certificates, documenting their learning experiences.
Dissemination activities will also be conducted, sharing the project outcomes within local communities. Lastly, WhatsApp
and Facebook groups will transition into alumni networks, helping to maintain continued communication and collaboration
among participants.
What measures will you put in place to ensure the safety and protection of participants?
Ensuring the safety and well-being of all participants is our top priority throughout every stage of the project. Therefore,
before the project begins, we will provide participants with a detailed infopack that includes critical safety-related
information. This document will outline the steps they need to take to ensure their well-being, such as obtaining a European
Health Insurance Card or travel insurance. Partner organizations will assist participants in acquiring these if needed. The
infopack will also include emergency contact numbers, local healthcare information, and practical advice on staying safe
during the program.
At the start of the project, there will be a comprehensive briefing where it will be ensured that all participants are aware of
the venue-specific rules, general safety guidelines, and local precautions. Topics such as responsible alcohol consumption
and venue security will be covered. Additionally, participants will collaboratively establish a set of project rules (during Code
of Conduct activity on the first day), emphasizing mutual respect, health, and safety.
To accommodate individual needs, we will gather information on participants’ special requirements, including dietary
restrictions and health conditions, in advance. This will be done through a pre-event survey or Google Form. With this data,
we will communicate with catering providers and make necessary arrangements to address these needs effectively. Rest
periods and coffee breaks will also be factored into the program to maintain participants’ mental and physical well-being.
Last but not least, accommodations will feature lockable rooms, while organizers will have a vehicle available to handle
emergencies, such as transportation to healthcare facilities or pharmacies.
What activities are foreseen after the end of the Youth Exchange? How will the participants follow-up on the activity?
After the Youth Exchange, participants will play a central role in sharing their experiences, using photos, videos, and
testimonials to highlight their journey on various social media platforms. Through their personal networks, they will raise
awareness of the project's themes and inspire others to engage with its ideas. Furthermore, depending on the dissemination
method participants will choose, they can also organize a local dissemination event, for at least 5 people, sharing the
experiences and learning outcomes they acquired from this project. Last but not least, participants will be encouraged to
maintain contact between them even after the end of the project, through their communication channels (WhatsApp,
Facebook, etc). This way, they will become ambassadors of the project, spreading its impact beyond the initial participants.
Meanwhile, the organizing team and partners will focus on disseminating the project outcomes. More specifically, the

Recognition of learning outcomes
How will you support participants to be aware of what they have learned and which competences they have developed or
improved? Please remember to include the methods that support reflection and documentation of the learning outcomes in
the daily timetable of each activity.
The “EQ for Inclusion” project integrates a variety of processes of structured reflection and documentation throughout its
implementation, in order to ensure participants are aware of their learning achievements and developed competences.
More specifically, through an interactive activity called “Escape room: the 8 keys” and later a workshop, participants will get
introduced even from the first day, to the Youthpass framework, its key competencies, and its purpose in tracking and
recognizing learning outcomes.
The significance of self-assessment and the value of documenting personal development will be pointed out from the
facilitators, while participants will set individual learning goals aligned with the project's themes, such as emotional
intelligence, inclusion, and intercultural understanding, that will guide their progress throughout the exchange. Early
activities such as “Expectation & Commitment art collage” will help participants map out their motivations, expectations, and
areas of personal growth using visual and interactive tools. These will serve as a baseline for evaluating their development.
In the last 5-10 minutes of each activity, participants will define together, with an interactive discussion, what was the aim of
the session, what did they learn and how can they use the knowledge they gained in the future.
Daily reflection will be a cornerstone of this program and therefore a three-step process will be featured. Firstly, there will be
individual reflection, where participants will engage in quiet self-assessment, using a daily reflection journal to document
what they learned, the challenges they encountered, and the skills they honed. In addition, there will be small group
discussions, where participants will regroup with their national teams -so that they feel more comfortable to express
themselves in their native language- to share reflections, focusing on highlights, challenges, and growth. The sessions will
be supported by the team leaders that will record the key points. Lastly, at the end of each day, team leaders will have a
meeting with the organizers to share feedback, address participant needs, and refine the program based on insights.
On the last day of the program, there will be “Reflection time & Evaluation” so that participants will engage firstly in
individual reflection, considering the total of personal growth and key takeaways they gained from the workshops and
activities and following, they will come together in small groups to share their reflections and outcomes. On the same day,
activities such as “Celebration and Sharing circle” and “Charting my EQ journey” will provide the participants the
opportunity to share meaningful moments, insights, and accomplishments from the program, creating a sense of community
and closure, but also reflect on their experiences and identify specific goals related to self-awareness, self-regulation,
empathy, and social skills.
Last but not least, a comprehensive booklet will be collaboratively created to highlight the project's key learnings, best
practices, and participant perspectives. This document will act as a lasting record of the initiative and will be uploaded to the
SALTO platform. By sharing, the project will provide valuable resources for other NGOs and youth workers, fostering
knowledge exchange and contributing to the wider Erasmus+ community.

Creating an inclusive environment is at the heart of our project, as diversity not only embraces equality but also enriches the

partners from Spain, Greece, and Italy, we will take targeted measures to support young people facing economic or social

Environmental friendly practices
Will you include sustainable and environmental-friendly practices in your activities?
Yes
Please describe them and mention how will you raise the awareness of participants on these sustainable practices?
Sustainability and environmentally friendly practices are key elements of our Erasmus+ youth exchange project and
therefore, green initiatives will be integrated into all aspects of the program.
During the project, raising awareness about these practices will be a priority. Even from the first day, sustainable habits will
be promoted, while actions like using reusable cups, switching off lights and water when not in use, minimizing food waste,
and correctly separating waste for recycling, will be emphasized.
At the project venue, single-use plastics will be avoided entirely. Instead, each participant will receive a reusable cup, which
they can label with their name and use throughout the project. Paper usage will be kept to a minimum, with a focus on using
recycled paper and printing on both sides when necessary.
Hungarian participants will arrive at the venue via public transport. This way, the project's overall carbon footprint will be
significantly reduced. All the participants will reach the venue from Budapest by train and bus.
Through these measures and discussions, we aim to encourage participants to adopt environmentally conscious practices
not only during the project but also in their everyday lives.

In this section we want you to describe several aspects of how you will manage your project and make sure that the

How will you manage the project (agreements with partners etc.) and make sure that it is done in line with the Erasmus

The project will be managed through close collaboration with our partner organizations, ensuring that all aspects are aligned
with the Erasmus+ Youth Quality Standards. To establish a structured and transparent approach, we will communicate
expectations, responsibilities, and key procedures well in advance. This will include clear guidelines on participant selection,
travel arrangements, accommodation details, and pre-departure preparation, ensuring that all partners are fully equipped to
support their participants.
A formal partnership agreement will be signed with each partner organization, outlining their specific roles and duties
throughout the project. This will cover essential areas such as the selection and preparation of participants, ensuring that
they meet the established criteria and are well-informed about the project’s objectives and logistics. The agreement will also
emphasize the importance of active participation in dissemination efforts, requiring each partner to contribute to the visibility
of the project by sharing materials and results within their networks. Additionally, each partner will be responsible for
assessing their national groups after the exchange, evaluating learning outcomes, personal growth, and the overall impact of
the experience.
To maintain efficient coordination, we will use shared digital tools such as Google Drive and communication platforms like

responsibilities, while additional meetings will take place during and after the exchange to monitor progress, address any

sense of ownership and commitment to the learning process. Additionally, to ensure an inclusive and supportive

Partnerships
How and why did you choose your project partners? What experiences and competences will they bring to the project?
The selection of our project partners was a carefully planned process, ensuring that each organization brings valuable
experience, expertise, and motivation to the project while also aligning with our objectives of promoting emotional
intelligence for inclusion.
Given that Square of Youth Association has less experience in organizing Erasmus+ projects, we deliberately sought
partners with strong backgrounds in youth work and international mobility projects. It was important for us to collaborate with
organizations that in their majority, have a track record of sending and preparing participants for such programs, ensuring
smooth project implementation.
The Spanish partner has extensive experience in Erasmus+ projects, having participated in numerous youth exchanges and
training courses. Their work focuses on youth empowerment, while one of their main goals is to give to the youth the
opportunity to improve their skills, experiences and competencies in different areas through the participation in international
projects, making them an excellent fit for our project. They also have a strong digital outreach strategy, which will be highly
beneficial for the dissemination phase, helping to spread the project’s results and impact beyond the immediate participants.
With the Spanish partner, we already had a good connection as we sent participants to their training course to Alcala La
Real in Spain in 2023 May, which we got the feedback from the participants that it was a really quality project.
The Greek partner consists of a dynamic team of young professionals dedicated to youth development and personal growth.
Their core mission is to create a world where every person cares and acts, aligning perfectly with our project’s objective of
equipping participants with EQ tools such as empathy, to bridge social and cultural divides and create inclusive
environments. Additionally, their members are actively engaged in grassroots initiatives that promote inclusion and
community-building, bringing fresh perspectives to the project. Their contribution will be instrumental in designing and
facilitating activities. The Greek partner is the most experienced among the partners, ensuring that they have the experience
being a great partner. They also hold a quality ESC label, which is another guarantee for us for the quality youth work.
Finally, the Italian partner shows a strong focus on fighting against discrimination and racism, while promoting the values of
solidarity, tolerance, and equality. Additionally, promoting social inclusion and empowering young people to discover their
own paths in life is one of their core objectives, aligning perfectly with this project’s initiatives and therefore becoming a
perfect fit for us. Their European focus and commitment to fostering inclusive spaces through non-formal education make
them a valuable contributor to the project. Even though this partner does not acquire great experience as wished, their
motivation and alignment to the project’s goals and objectives made them an ideal partner. We have an extensive work
relation with the Italian partner as they include us in their KA154 project about fast fashion.
Before submitting the project, we conducted online meetings with all three partners to assess their motivation, discuss their
potential contributions, and ensure their commitment to the project’s success. These discussions confirmed that each
organization is well-prepared and highly motivated to contribute meaningfully to the project, not only by sending participants
but also by actively supporting the implementation, evaluation, and dissemination of results.
How will you communicate with them?
To ensure smooth and efficient communication with the project partners, two WhatsApp groups will be created: one for
partner organization representatives and team leaders, and another for project participants. The group for partners will be
created during the preparation phase, while the participants' group will be set up a few weeks before the project begins.
Both groups will remain active throughout the implementation and follow-up phases, allowing for continuous updates and
coordination.
Throughout the planning stage, we have been communicating with our partners through emails. This communication will go
on and become even more frequent as the project approaches—especially when finalizing travel arrangements. Alongside
emails, key updates and reminders will also be shared via WhatsApp to ensure all important information reaches everyone
on time.
Before the project starts, an online preparatory meeting will be organized with partner representatives, team leaders, and
the organizing team. This meeting will cover all important details such as: project details, participant selection criteria, travel
guidelines, the responsibilities of team leaders, accommodation arrangements, a review of the schedule, while any concerns
will be addressed too.
Once the project concludes, we will hold a final online meeting with partners and team leaders to evaluate the experience,
reflect on challenges and successes, and discuss possibilities for future collaboration.
Communication will continue beyond the project itself. Both WhatsApp groups will remain open to facilitate ongoing
discussions, final reporting, reimbursements, and any necessary follow-up tasks, ensuring a smooth transition into the post-
project phase.
How will you monitor and coordinate their contribution?
To ensure clear communication and effective coordination, each partner will have a well-defined set of responsibilities,

personal skills but also gain real-world experience in applying emotional intelligence to create inclusive environments.

Evaluation
How will you evaluate your project’s success? Which activities will you carry out in order to assess whether, and to what
extent, your project has reached its objectives and results?
To evaluate the success of our project effectively, we will use a combination of tools to measure participant engagement,
knowledge growth, and emotional intelligence development at three key stages: before, during, and after the project.
Additionally, we will incorporate objective indicators to assess participation levels, engagement, and the effectiveness of
dissemination efforts.
Before the Project: Even before the project begins, we will administer a questionnaire to assess participants’ initial
knowledge and understanding of emotional intelligence and its role in promoting inclusion. The questionnaire will focus on
participants' understanding of EQ, the EQ skills they have possibly cultivated prior to the project, and, last but not least, their
knowledge of using EQ for creating inclusive environments. This will establish a baseline for measuring their progress
throughout the project. We will also ensure logistical preparedness that 100% of participants arrive at the venue on time and
have completed their preparatory tasks before departure.
During the Project: Throughout the project, after each session, we will hold open discussions with participants to reflect on
the aims of the activity, what they have learned, and their overall satisfaction. This immediate feedback will help us adjust
activities to ensure they align with the intended learning outcomes. Additionally, daily reflection (personal and in groups) will
give participants the opportunity to understand and share thoughts, insights, and challenges. These sessions embrace open
dialogue about the effectiveness of the project and ensure it remains responsive to the participants’ needs.
In addition to participant feedback, we will gather insights from group leaders. Each evening, we will meet with the group

environments. To ensure that the impact doesn’t fade, we will post regularly -every couple of weeks- keeping the content in

more individuals and groups to engage with the key principles of emotional intelligence for inclusion.

alumni groups. Participants will be able to interact and support each other’s ongoing journeys, celebrate each other's

document and share the project's activities, milestones, and participant testimonials. These platforms will help us engage

how a youth exchange unfolds. We will post short videos featuring reflections from participants on how emotional

workshops, explaining how developing emotional intelligence can help young people navigate challenges related to mental

To further amplify our reach, we will utilize relevant hashtags that connect the project to broader conversations around
emotional intelligence, mental health, and youth development. Our goal is to engage at least 400 individuals through direct
